-- | The CEK machine is parameterized over a <tt>spendBudget</tt> function.
--   This makes the budgeting machinery extensible and allows us to
--   separate budgeting logic from evaluation logic and avoid branching on
--   the union of all possible budgeting state types during evaluation.
newtype CekBudgetSpender (uni :: Type -> Type) fun s
CekBudgetSpender :: (ExBudgetCategory fun -> ExBudget -> CekM uni fun s ()) -> CekBudgetSpender (uni :: Type -> Type) fun s
[unCekBudgetSpender] :: CekBudgetSpender (uni :: Type -> Type) fun s -> ExBudgetCategory fun -> ExBudget -> CekM uni fun s ()

-- | A budgeting mode to execute the CEK machine in.
newtype ExBudgetMode cost (uni :: Type -> Type) fun
ExBudgetMode :: (forall s. () => ST s (ExBudgetInfo cost uni fun s)) -> ExBudgetMode cost (uni :: Type -> Type) fun
[unExBudgetMode] :: ExBudgetMode cost (uni :: Type -> Type) fun -> forall s. () => ST s (ExBudgetInfo cost uni fun s)
data StepKind
BConst :: StepKind
BVar :: StepKind
BLamAbs :: StepKind
BApply :: StepKind
BDelay :: StepKind
BForce :: StepKind
BBuiltin :: StepKind
BConstr :: StepKind
BCase :: StepKind

-- | For a detailed report on what costs how much + the same overall budget
--   that <tt>Counting</tt> gives. The (derived) <a>Monoid</a> instance of
--   <a>CekExTally</a> is the main piece of the machinery.
newtype CekExTally fun
CekExTally :: MonoidalHashMap (ExBudgetCategory fun) ExBudget -> CekExTally fun

-- | For calculating the cost of execution by counting up using the
--   <a>Monoid</a> instance of <a>ExBudget</a>.
newtype CountingSt
CountingSt :: ExBudget -> CountingSt
data TallyingSt fun
TallyingSt :: CekExTally fun -> ExBudget -> TallyingSt fun
newtype RestrictingSt
RestrictingSt :: ExRestrictingBudget -> RestrictingSt

-- | Costs for evaluating AST nodes. Times should be specified in
--   picoseconds, memory sizes in bytes.
type CekMachineCosts = CekMachineCostsBase Identity

-- | For calculating the cost of execution.
counting :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. ExBudgetMode CountingSt uni fun

-- | For a detailed report on what costs how much + the same overall budget
--   that <tt>Counting</tt> gives.
tallying :: forall fun (uni :: Type -> Type). Hashable fun => ExBudgetMode (TallyingSt fun) uni fun

-- | For execution, to avoid overruns.
restricting :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. ThrowableBuiltins uni fun => ExRestrictingBudget -> ExBudgetMode RestrictingSt uni fun

-- | <a>restricting</a> instantiated at <a>enormousBudget</a>.
restrictingEnormous :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. ThrowableBuiltins uni fun => ExBudgetMode RestrictingSt uni fun

-- | When we want to just evaluate the program we use the
--   <tt>Restricting</tt> mode with an enormous budget, so that evaluation
--   costs of on-chain budgeting are reflected accurately in benchmarks.
enormousBudget :: ExRestrictingBudget

-- | No emitter.
noEmitter :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. EmitterMode uni fun

-- | Emits log only.
logEmitter :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. EmitterMode uni fun

-- | Emits log with timestamp.
logWithTimeEmitter :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. EmitterMode uni fun

-- | Emits log with the budget.
logWithBudgetEmitter :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. EmitterMode uni fun

-- | Emits log and, when script evaluation fails, call trace.
--   
--   This requires script to be compiled with `Plinth.Plugin:profile-all`
--   turned on because this relies on compiler-generated trace calls that
--   notifies entrance and exit of a function call. These traces that mark
--   entrance and exit are ordinary traces like "-&gt;
--   rob:Example.hs:3:1-3:15" and "&lt;- bob:Example.hs:1:1-1:13" with
--   "-&gt;" and "&lt;-" prefixies, where "bob" and "rob" is the name of
--   the function with source span. If regular script with no entrance/exit
--   marker is given, this emitter will behave identically to
--   <a>logEmitter</a>.
--   
--   When script evaluation fails, this emitter will give call trace of the
--   functions that led to the evaluation failure. This is useful for
--   pin-pointing specific area of the codebase that caused failure when
--   debugging a script. When script evaluation passes, every trace message
--   generated by `profile-all` flag will be removed, and this emitter will
--   behave identically to <a>logEmitter</a>.
logWithCallTraceEmitter :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. EmitterMode uni fun
data CekValue (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
VCon :: !Some (ValueOf uni) -> CekValue (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
VDelay :: !NTerm uni fun ann -> !CekValEnv uni fun ann -> CekValue (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
VLamAbs :: !NamedDeBruijn -> !NTerm uni fun ann -> !CekValEnv uni fun ann -> CekValue (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann

-- | A partial builtin application, accumulating arguments for eventual
--   full application. We don't need a <a>CekValEnv</a> here unlike in the
--   other constructors, because <a>VBuiltin</a> values always store their
--   corresponding <a>Term</a>s fully discharged, see the comments at the
--   call sites (search for <a>VBuiltin</a>).
VBuiltin :: !fun -> NTerm uni fun () -> !BuiltinRuntime (CekValue uni fun ann) -> CekValue (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
VConstr :: {-# UNPACK #-} !Word64 -> !EmptyOrMultiStack uni fun ann -> CekValue (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann

-- | Unlift a value using the Steppable CEK machine. *THIS FUNCTION IS
--   PARTIAL if the input term contains free variables*
readKnownCek :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a ann. (ThrowableBuiltins uni fun, ReadKnown (Term Name uni fun ()) a) => MachineParameters CekMachineCosts fun (CekValue uni fun ann) -> Term Name uni fun ann -> Either (CekEvaluationException Name uni fun) a
class Eq a => Hashable a

-- | The set of constraints we need to be able to throw exceptions with
--   things with built-in types and functions in them.
type ThrowableBuiltins (uni :: Type -> Type) fun = (PrettyUni uni, Pretty fun, Typeable uni, Typeable fun)

module UntypedPlutusCore.Mark

-- | Marks all the <a>Unique</a>s in a term as used, so they will not be
--   generated in future. Useful if you have a term which was not generated
--   in <a>Quote</a>.
markNonFreshTerm :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann m. (HasUniques (Term name uni fun ann), MonadQuote m) => Term name uni fun ann -> m ()

-- | Marks all the <a>Unique</a>s in a program as used, so they will not be
--   generated in future. Useful if you have a program which was not
--   generated in <a>Quote</a>.
markNonFreshProgram :: forall name m (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann. (HasUnique name TermUnique, MonadQuote m) => Program name uni fun ann -> m ()

module UntypedPlutusCore.MkUPlc

-- | An untyped "variable declaration", i.e. a name for a variable.
data UVarDecl name ann
UVarDecl :: ann -> name -> UVarDecl name ann
[_uvarDeclAnn] :: UVarDecl name ann -> ann
[_uvarDeclName] :: UVarDecl name ann -> name
uvarDeclName :: forall name1 ann name2 f. Functor f => (name1 -> f name2) -> UVarDecl name1 ann -> f (UVarDecl name2 ann)
uvarDeclAnn :: forall name ann1 ann2 f. Functor f => (ann1 -> f ann2) -> UVarDecl name ann1 -> f (UVarDecl name ann2)

-- | Make a <a>Var</a> referencing the given <a>UVarDecl</a>. The
--   <tt>ann</tt> is propagated from the <a>UVarDecl</a> to the <a>Var</a>.
mkVar :: forall name ann (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. UVarDecl name ann -> Term name uni fun ann

-- | Lambda abstract a list of names.
mkIterLamAbs :: forall name ann (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. [UVarDecl name ann] -> Term name uni fun ann -> Term name uni fun ann

-- | A definition. Pretty much just a pair with more descriptive names.
data Def var val
Def :: var -> val -> Def var val
[defVar] :: Def var val -> var
[defVal] :: Def var val -> val

-- | A term definition as a variable.
type UTermDef name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann = Def UVarDecl name ann Term name uni fun ann

module PlutusCore.Compiler.Opts
data CompilationOpts name fun a
CompilationOpts :: OptimizeOpts name a -> BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> CompilationOpts name fun a
[_coOptimizeOpts] :: CompilationOpts name fun a -> OptimizeOpts name a
[_coBuiltinSemanticsVariant] :: CompilationOpts name fun a -> BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun
coOptimizeOpts :: forall name1 fun a1 name2 a2 f. Functor f => (OptimizeOpts name1 a1 -> f (OptimizeOpts name2 a2)) -> CompilationOpts name1 fun a1 -> f (CompilationOpts name2 fun a2)
coBuiltinSemanticsVariant :: forall name fun1 a fun2 f. Functor f => (BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun1 -> f (BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun2)) -> CompilationOpts name fun1 a -> f (CompilationOpts name fun2 a)
defaultCompilationOpts :: Default (BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun) => CompilationOpts name fun a

module UntypedPlutusCore.Purity

-- | Will evaluating this term have side effects (looping or error)? This
--   is slightly wider than the definition of a value, as it includes
--   applications that are known to be pure, as well as things that can't
--   be returned from the machine (as they'd be ill-scoped).
isPure :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun name a. ToBuiltinMeaning uni fun => BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Term name uni fun a -> Bool

-- | Is the given term 'work-free'?
--   
--   Note: The definition of 'work-free' is a little unclear, but the idea
--   is that evaluating this term should do very a trivial amount of work.
isWorkFree :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun name a. ToBuiltinMeaning uni fun => BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Term name uni fun a -> Bool

-- | The order in which terms get evaluated, along with their purities.
data EvalOrder name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a

-- | Get the evaluation order as a list of <a>EvalTerm</a>s. Either
--   terminates in a single <a>Unknown</a>, which means that we got to a
--   point where evaluation continues but we don't know where; or
--   terminates normally, in which case we actually got to the end of the
--   evaluation order for the term.
unEvalOrder :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. EvalOrder name uni fun a -> [EvalTerm name uni fun a]

-- | Either the "next" term to be evaluated, along with its <a>Purity</a>
--   and <a>WorkFreedom</a>, or we don't know what comes next.
data EvalTerm name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
Unknown :: EvalTerm name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
EvalTerm :: Purity -> WorkFreedom -> Term name uni fun a -> EvalTerm name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a

-- | Is this pure? Either yes, or maybe not.
data Purity
MaybeImpure :: Purity
Pure :: Purity

-- | Is this term essentially work-free? Either yes, or maybe not.
data WorkFreedom
MaybeWork :: WorkFreedom
WorkFree :: WorkFreedom

-- | Given a term, return the order in which it and its sub-terms will be
--   evaluated.
--   
--   This aims to be a sound under-approximation: if we don't know, we just
--   say <a>Unknown</a>. Typically there will be a sequence of terms that
--   we do know, which will terminate in <a>Unknown</a> once we do
--   something like call a function.
--   
--   This makes some assumptions about the evaluator, in particular about
--   the order in which we evaluate sub-terms, but these match the current
--   evaluator and we are not planning on changing it.
termEvaluationOrder :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. ToBuiltinMeaning uni fun => BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Term name uni fun a -> EvalOrder name uni fun a
instance GHC.Classes.Eq (UntypedPlutusCore.Core.Type.Term name uni fun a) => GHC.Classes.Eq (UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.EvalTerm name uni fun a)
instance GHC.Classes.Eq UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.Purity
instance GHC.Classes.Eq UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.WorkFreedom
instance GHC.Base.Monoid (UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.EvalOrder name uni fun a)
instance Text.PrettyBy.Internal.PrettyBy config (UntypedPlutusCore.Core.Type.Term name uni fun a) => Text.PrettyBy.Internal.PrettyBy config (UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.EvalOrder name uni fun a)
instance Text.PrettyBy.Internal.PrettyBy config (UntypedPlutusCore.Core.Type.Term name uni fun a) => Text.PrettyBy.Internal.PrettyBy config (UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.EvalTerm name uni fun a)
instance Prettyprinter.Internal.Pretty UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.Purity
instance Prettyprinter.Internal.Pretty UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.WorkFreedom
instance GHC.Base.Semigroup (UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.EvalOrder name uni fun a)
instance (GHC.Show.Show name, Universe.Core.Everywhere uni GHC.Show.Show, GHC.Show.Show fun, GHC.Show.Show a, Data.GADT.Internal.GShow uni, Universe.Core.Closed uni) => GHC.Show.Show (UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.EvalTerm name uni fun a)
instance GHC.Show.Show UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.Purity
instance GHC.Show.Show UntypedPlutusCore.Purity.WorkFreedom


-- | The internal module of the renamer that defines the actual algorithms,
--   but not the user-facing API.
module UntypedPlutusCore.Rename.Internal

-- | Rename a <a>Term</a> in the <tt>RenameM</tt> monad.
renameTermM :: forall m name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann. (MonadRename m, HasUniques (Term name uni fun ann)) => Term name uni fun ann -> m (Term name uni fun ann)

-- | Rename a <a>Program</a> in the <tt>RenameM</tt> monad.
renameProgramM :: forall m name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann. (MonadRename m, HasUniques (Program name uni fun ann)) => Program name uni fun ann -> m (Program name uni fun ann)


-- | The user-facing API of the untyped renamer. See PlutusCore.Rename for
--   details.
module UntypedPlutusCore.Rename

-- | The class of things that can be renamed. I.e. things that are capable
--   of satisfying the global uniqueness condition.
class Rename a

-- | Rename <a>Unique</a>s so that they're globally unique. In case there
--   are any free variables, they must be left untouched and bound
--   variables must not get renamed to free ones. Must always assign new
--   names to bound variables, so that <tt>rename</tt> can be used for
--   alpha-renaming as well.
rename :: (Rename a, MonadQuote m) => a -> m a
instance PlutusCore.Core.Type.HasUniques (UntypedPlutusCore.Core.Type.Program name uni fun ann) => PlutusCore.Rename.Rename (UntypedPlutusCore.Core.Type.Program name uni fun ann)
instance PlutusCore.Core.Type.HasUniques (UntypedPlutusCore.Core.Type.Term name uni fun ann) => PlutusCore.Rename.Rename (UntypedPlutusCore.Core.Type.Term name uni fun ann)

module UntypedPlutusCore.Parser
parse :: (MonadError ParserErrorBundle m, MonadQuote m) => Parser a -> String -> Text -> m a

-- | Parser for all UPLC terms.
term :: Parser PTerm

-- | Parser for UPLC programs.
program :: Parser (Program Name DefaultUni DefaultFun SrcSpan)

-- | Parse a UPLC term. The resulting program will have fresh names. The
--   underlying monad must be capable of handling any parse errors.
parseTerm :: (MonadError ParserErrorBundle m, MonadQuote m) => Text -> m PTerm

-- | Parse a UPLC program. The resulting program will have fresh names. The
--   underlying monad must be capable of handling any parse errors. This
--   passes "test" to the parser as the name of the input stream; to supply
--   a name explicity, use `parse program <a>name</a> <a>input</a><a>.</a>
parseProgram :: (MonadError ParserErrorBundle m, MonadQuote m) => Text -> m (Program Name DefaultUni DefaultFun SrcSpan)

-- | Parse and rewrite so that names are globally unique, not just unique
--   within their scope.
parseScoped :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun m. (MonadError (Error uni fun SrcSpan) m, MonadQuote m) => Text -> m (Program Name DefaultUni DefaultFun SrcSpan)
type Parser = ParsecT ParserError Text StateT ParserState ReaderT Maybe Version Quote
data SourcePos

module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Hints

-- | Certifier hints for the inlining pass.
data Inline
InlVar :: Inline
InlLam :: Inline -> Inline
InlApply :: Inline -> Inline -> Inline
InlForce :: Inline -> Inline
InlDelay :: Inline -> Inline
InlCon :: Inline
InlBuiltin :: Inline
InlError :: Inline
InlConstr :: [Inline] -> Inline
InlCase :: Inline -> [Inline] -> Inline
InlExpand :: Inline -> Inline
InlDrop :: Inline -> Inline

-- | Hints for the certifier.
--   
--   Note that there's a separate and unrelated notion of
--   <tt>InlineHints</tt>. When there's ambiguity, this should be referred
--   to as "certifier hints".
data Hints
Inline :: Inline -> Hints
NoHints :: Hints
instance GHC.Generics.Generic UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Hints.Hints
instance GHC.Generics.Generic UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Hints.Inline
instance Control.DeepSeq.NFData UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Hints.Hints
instance Control.DeepSeq.NFData UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Hints.Inline

module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Trace

-- | Datatype which represents optimization passes which are also
--   certified.
--   
--   This means that these passes are formalized as part of the certifier,
--   and adding a new pass constructor to this type means that it is
--   expected the pass will be also certified in the same PR.
--   
--   WARNING: the order of the constructors MUST be the same as the order
--   of their counterparts in <a>Trace</a>.
data CertifiedOptStage
FloatDelay :: CertifiedOptStage
ForceDelay :: CertifiedOptStage
ForceCaseDelay :: CertifiedOptStage
Inline :: CertifiedOptStage
CSE :: CertifiedOptStage
ApplyToCase :: CertifiedOptStage
CaseReduce :: CertifiedOptStage
LetFloatOut :: CertifiedOptStage

-- | Datatype which represents optimization passes which are not yet
--   certified.
--   
--   WARNING: the order of the constructors MUST be the same as the order
--   of their counterparts in <a>Trace</a>.
--   
--   IMPORTANT: if you add a new pass, or modify an existing pass, without
--   also modifying the certifier in the same PR, you must add/move its
--   corresponding constructor to this type. Please also open an issue at
--   <a>https://github.com/IntersectMBO/plutus/issues</a>.
data UncertifiedOptStage
CaseOfCase :: UncertifiedOptStage
ConstantFolding :: UncertifiedOptStage
PolyBuiltin :: UncertifiedOptStage
type OptStage = Either UncertifiedOptStage CertifiedOptStage
pattern FloatDelayStage :: OptStage
pattern ForceDelayStage :: OptStage
pattern ForceCaseDelayStage :: OptStage
pattern CaseReduceStage :: OptStage
pattern InlineStage :: OptStage
pattern CseStage :: OptStage
pattern ApplyToCaseStage :: OptStage
pattern CaseOfCaseStage :: OptStage
pattern LetFloatOutStage :: OptStage
pattern ConstantFoldingStage :: OptStage
pattern PolyBuiltinStage :: OptStage
data Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
Optimization :: Term name uni fun a -> OptStage -> Hints -> Term name uni fun a -> Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
[beforeAST] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> Term name uni fun a
[stage] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> OptStage
[hints] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> Hints
[afterAST] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> Term name uni fun a
newtype OptimizerTrace name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
OptimizerTrace :: [Optimization name uni fun a] -> OptimizerTrace name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
[optimizerTrace] :: OptimizerTrace name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> [Optimization name uni fun a]
initOptimizerTrace :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. OptimizerTrace name uni fun a
allASTs :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. OptimizerTrace name uni fun a -> [Term name uni fun a]
instance GHC.Generics.Generic UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Trace.CertifiedOptStage
instance GHC.Generics.Generic UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Trace.UncertifiedOptStage
instance Control.DeepSeq.NFData UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Trace.CertifiedOptStage
instance Control.DeepSeq.NFData UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Trace.UncertifiedOptStage
instance GHC.Show.Show UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Trace.CertifiedOptStage
instance GHC.Show.Show UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Certify.Trace.UncertifiedOptStage

module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Optimizer
newtype OptimizerT name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann (m :: Type -> Type) a
OptimizerT :: StateT (OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann) m a -> OptimizerT name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann (m :: Type -> Type) a
[getOptimizerT] :: OptimizerT name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann (m :: Type -> Type) a -> StateT (OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann) m a
type OptStage = Either UncertifiedOptStage CertifiedOptStage
pattern FloatDelayStage :: OptStage
pattern ForceDelayStage :: OptStage
pattern ForceCaseDelayStage :: OptStage
pattern CaseReduceStage :: OptStage
pattern InlineStage :: OptStage
pattern CseStage :: OptStage
pattern ApplyToCaseStage :: OptStage
pattern CaseOfCaseStage :: OptStage
pattern LetFloatOutStage :: OptStage
pattern ConstantFoldingStage :: OptStage
pattern PolyBuiltinStage :: OptStage
newtype OptimizerTrace name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
OptimizerTrace :: [Optimization name uni fun a] -> OptimizerTrace name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
[optimizerTrace] :: OptimizerTrace name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> [Optimization name uni fun a]
data Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
Optimization :: Term name uni fun a -> OptStage -> Hints -> Term name uni fun a -> Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
[beforeAST] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> Term name uni fun a
[stage] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> OptStage
[hints] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> Hints
[afterAST] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> Term name uni fun a
runOptimizerT :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann m a. OptimizerT name uni fun ann m a -> m (a, OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann)
evalOptimizerT :: forall m name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann a. Monad m => OptimizerT name uni fun ann m a -> m a
execOptimizerT :: forall m name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann a. Monad m => OptimizerT name uni fun ann m a -> m (OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann)
type Optimizer name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann = OptimizerT name uni fun ann Identity
runOptimizer :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann a. Optimizer name uni fun ann a -> (a, OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann)
evalOptimizer :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann a. Optimizer name uni fun ann a -> a
execOptimizer :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann a. Optimizer name uni fun ann a -> OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann
initOptimizerTrace :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. OptimizerTrace name uni fun a
recordOptimization :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. Monad m => Term name uni fun a -> OptStage -> Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m ()
recordOptimizationWithHints :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. Monad m => Hints -> Term name uni fun a -> OptStage -> Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m ()
instance GHC.Base.Monad m => GHC.Base.Applicative (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Optimizer.OptimizerT name uni fun ann m)
instance GHC.Base.Functor m => GHC.Base.Functor (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Optimizer.OptimizerT name uni fun ann m)
instance GHC.Base.Monad m => GHC.Base.Monad (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Optimizer.OptimizerT name uni fun ann m)
instance PlutusCore.Quote.MonadQuote m => PlutusCore.Quote.MonadQuote (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Optimizer.OptimizerT name uni fun ann m)
instance Control.Monad.Trans.Class.MonadTrans (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Optimizer.OptimizerT name uni fun ann)


-- | Floats immediately-applied lambdas ("let bindings") outwards, as long
--   as doing so cannot cause any expression to be evaluated more than
--   before.
--   
--   This can unlock further optimizations, such as case-constr and
--   force-delay cancellation.
--   
--   Specifically, it floats bindings from the following positions:
--   <tt>case</tt> scrutinee, <tt>force</tt> body, and the function of
--   <tt>apply</tt>.
--   
--   If we don't care about the order of effects, we can also float
--   bindings from <tt>apply</tt> arguments and <tt>constr</tt> arguments,
--   but there's no evidence that doing so is beneficial, so we don't do
--   it.
--   
--   This pass only floats one bindings from each location. Since the pass
--   runs once per simplifier iteration, this should be sufficient.
--   Floating out multi-lets at once would complicate things, since we'd
--   need to consider both <tt>(a b. M) A B</tt> and <tt>(a. (b -&gt; M) B)
--   A</tt>.
module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.LetFloatOut
letFloatOut :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. (MonadQuote m, Rename (Term name uni fun a)) => Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m (Term name uni fun a)


-- | An inlining pass.
--   
--   This pass is essentially a copy of the PIR inliner, and should be KEPT
--   IN SYNC with it. It's hard to do this with true abstraction, so we
--   just have to keep two copies reasonably similar.
--   
--   However, there are some differences. In the interests of making it
--   easier to keep things in sync, these are explicitly listed in Note
--   [Differences from PIR inliner]. If you add another difference, please
--   note it there! Obviously fewer differences is better.
--   
--   See Note [The problem of inlining destructors] for why this pass
--   exists.
module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Inline

-- | Inline simple bindings. Relies on global uniqueness, and preserves it.
--   See Note [Inlining and global uniqueness]
inline :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun (m :: Type -> Type) a. ExternalConstraints name uni fun m => AstSize -> AstSize -> Bool -> Bool -> InlineHints name a -> BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m (Term name uni fun a)
newtype InlineHints name a
InlineHints :: (a -> name -> Inline) -> InlineHints name a
[shouldInline] :: InlineHints name a -> a -> name -> Inline

-- | The monad the inliner runs in.
type InlineM name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a = ReaderT InlineInfo name fun a StateT S name uni fun Ann a Quote

-- | UPLC inliner state
data S name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
S :: Subst name uni fun a -> UniqueMap TermUnique (VarInfo name uni fun a) -> S name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
[_subst] :: S name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> Subst name uni fun a
[_vars] :: S name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> UniqueMap TermUnique (VarInfo name uni fun a)

-- | An enclosing Drop or Expand layer
data Decoration
DDrop :: Decoration
DExpand :: Decoration
type Ann a = ([Decoration], a)
data InlineInfo name fun a
InlineInfo :: Usages -> InlineHints name a -> BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Bool -> AstSize -> AstSize -> Bool -> InlineInfo name fun a
[_iiUsages] :: InlineInfo name fun a -> Usages
[_iiHints] :: InlineInfo name fun a -> InlineHints name a
[_iiBuiltinSemanticsVariant] :: InlineInfo name fun a -> BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun
[_iiInlineConstants] :: InlineInfo name fun a -> Bool
[_iiInlineUnconditionalGrowth] :: InlineInfo name fun a -> AstSize
[_iiInlineCallsiteGrowth] :: InlineInfo name fun a -> AstSize
[_iiPreserveLogging] :: InlineInfo name fun a -> Bool

-- | Wrapper of term substitution so that it's similar to the PIR inliner.
--   See Note [Differences from PIR inliner] 1
newtype Subst name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
Subst :: TermEnv name uni fun a -> Subst name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
[_termEnv] :: Subst name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> TermEnv name uni fun a

-- | Term substitution, <a>Subst</a> in the paper. A map of unprocessed
--   variable and its substitution range.
newtype TermEnv name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
TermEnv :: UniqueMap TermUnique (InlineTerm name uni fun a) -> TermEnv name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
[_unTermEnv] :: TermEnv name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> UniqueMap TermUnique (InlineTerm name uni fun a)
isFirstVarBeforeEffects :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann. InliningConstraints name uni fun => BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> name -> Term name uni fun ann -> Bool

-- | Check if the given name is strict in the given term. This means that
--   at least one occurrence of the name is found outside of the following:
--   * <tt>delay</tt> term * lambda body * case branch
isStrictIn :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. Eq name => name -> Term name uni fun a -> Bool
effectSafe :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a b. InliningConstraints name uni fun => Term name uni fun a -> name -> Bool -> InlineM name uni fun b Bool
instance GHC.Generics.Generic (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Inline.Subst name uni fun a)
instance GHC.Base.Monoid (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Inline.S name uni fun a)
instance GHC.Base.Monoid (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Inline.Subst name uni fun a)
instance GHC.Base.Monoid (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Inline.TermEnv name uni fun a)
instance GHC.Base.Semigroup (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Inline.S name uni fun a)
instance GHC.Base.Semigroup (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Inline.Subst name uni fun a)
instance GHC.Base.Semigroup (UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Inline.TermEnv name uni fun a)

module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.ForceDelay

-- | Traverses the term, for each node applying the optimisation detailed
--   above. For implementation details see <a>optimisationProcedure</a>.
forceDelay :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun (m :: Type -> Type) name a. (uni ~ DefaultUni, fun ~ DefaultFun, Monad m) => BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m (Term name uni fun a)

module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.ForceCaseDelay
forceCaseDelay :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. Monad m => Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m (Term name uni fun a)


-- | The Float Delay optimization floats <a>Delay</a> from arguments into
--   function bodies, if possible. It turns <tt>(n -&gt; ...Force n...Force
--   n...) (Delay arg)</tt> into <tt>(n -&gt; ...Force (Delay n)...Force
--   (Delay n)...) arg</tt>.
--   
--   The above transformation is performed if:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>All occurrences of <tt>arg</tt> are under <tt>Force</tt>.</li>
--   <li><tt>arg</tt> is essentially work-free.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   This achieves a similar effect to Plutonomy's "Split Delay"
--   optimization. The difference is that Split Delay simply splits the
--   <tt>Delay</tt> argument into multiple arguments, turning the above
--   example into <tt>(m -&gt; (n -&gt; ...Force n...Force n...) (Delay m))
--   arg</tt>, and then relies on other optimizations to simplify it
--   further. Specifically, once the inliner inlines <tt>Delay m</tt>, it
--   will be identical to the result of Float Delay.
--   
--   The advantages of Float Delay are:
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>It doesn't rely on the inliner. In this example, Split Delay
--   relies on the inliner to inline <tt>Delay m</tt>, but there's no
--   guarantee that the inliner will do so, because inlining it may
--   increase the program size.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   We can potentially modify the inliner such that it is aware of Float
--   Delay and Force-Delay Cancel, and makes inlining decisions with these
--   other optimizations in mind. The problem is that, not only does this
--   makes the inlining heuristics much more complex, but it could easily
--   lead to code duplication. Other optimizations often need to do some
--   calculation in order to make certain optimization decisions (e.g., in
--   this case, we want to check whether all occurrences of <tt>arg</tt>
--   are under <tt>Force</tt>), and if we rely on the inliner to inline the
--   <tt>Delay</tt>, then the same check would need to be performed by the
--   inliner.
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>Because Force Delay requires that all occurrences of <tt>arg</tt>
--   are under <tt>Force</tt>, it guarantees to not increase the size or
--   the cost of the program. This is not the case with Split Delay: in
--   this example, if the occurrences of <tt>n</tt> are not under
--   <tt>Force</tt>, then Split Delay may increase the size of the program,
--   regardless of whether or not <tt>Delay m</tt> is inlined. If <tt>Delay
--   m</tt> is not inlined, then it will also increase the cost of the
--   program, due to the additional application.</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   The alternative approach that always floats the <tt>Delay</tt>
--   regardless of whether or not all occurences of <tt>arg</tt> are under
--   <tt>Force</tt> was implemented and tested, and it is strictly worse
--   than Float Delay on our current test suite (specifically, Split Delay
--   causes one test case to have a slightly bigger program, and everything
--   else is equal).
--   
--   Why is this optimization performed on UPLC, not PIR?
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>Not only are the types and let-bindings in PIR not useful for this
--   optimization, they can also get in the way. For example, we cannot
--   transform <tt>let f = /a. ...a... in ...{f t1}...{f t2}...</tt> into
--   <tt>ket f = ...a... in ...f...f...</tt>.</li>
--   <li>This optimization mainly interacts with ForceDelayCancel and the
--   inliner, and both are part of the UPLC simplifier.</li>
--   </ol>
module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.FloatDelay
floatDelay :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. (MonadQuote m, Rename (Term name uni fun a), HasUnique name TermUnique) => Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m (Term name uni fun a)


-- | This module mirrors <a>EvaluateBuiltins</a>, adapted for UPLC's
--   untyped <a>Term</a> and <a>AppCtx</a>. See that module for detailed
--   commentary.
module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.EvaluateBuiltins
evaluateBuiltinsPass :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) (uni :: Type -> Type) fun name a. (Monad m, ToBuiltinMeaning uni fun, Typeable name) => Bool -> BuiltinsInfo uni fun -> CostingPart uni fun -> Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m (Term name uni fun a)

module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.Cse
cse :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann. (MonadQuote m, Hashable (Term Name uni fun ()), Rename (Term Name uni fun ann), ToBuiltinMeaning uni fun) => CseWhichSubterms -> BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Term Name uni fun ann -> OptimizerT Name uni fun ann m (Term Name uni fun ann)

module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.CaseReduce
caseReduce :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) (uni :: Type -> Type) name fun a. (Monad m, CaseBuiltin uni) => Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m (Term name uni fun a)
processTerm :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) name fun a. CaseBuiltin uni => Term name uni fun a -> Term name uni fun a


-- | Perform the case-of-case transformation. This pushes case expressions
--   into the case branches of other case expressions, which can often
--   yield optimization opportunities.
--   
--   Example: @ case (case s of { C1 a -&gt; x; C2 b -&gt; y; }) of D1
--   -&gt; w D2 -&gt; z
--   
--   <ul>
--   <li>-&gt;</li>
--   </ul>
--   
--   case s of C1 a -&gt; case x of { D1 -&gt; w; D2 -&gt; z; } C2 b -&gt;
--   case y of { D1 -&gt; w; D2 -&gt; z; } @
--   
--   We also transform
--   
--   <pre>
--   case ((force ifThenElse) b (constr t) (constr f)) alts
--   </pre>
--   
--   into
--   
--   <pre>
--   force (force ifThenElse b (delay (case (constr t) alts)) (delay (case (constr f) alts)))
--   </pre>
--   
--   This is always an improvement.
module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.CaseOfCase
caseOfCase :: forall fun (m :: Type -> Type) (uni :: Type -> Type) name a. (fun ~ DefaultFun, Monad m, CaseBuiltin uni, GEq uni, Closed uni, Everywhere uni Eq) => Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m (Term name uni fun a)


-- | Transform multi-argument applications into case-constr form.
--   
--   This is essentially the reverse of <tt>CaseReduce</tt>. An application
--   of 3 or more arguments, e.g., <tt>f x y z</tt> is rewritten to
--   <tt>case (constr 0 [x, y, z]) [f]</tt>, which is cheaper, and smaller
--   in AST size, though it is sometimes slightly bigger in cbor size.
--   
--   I cannot think of any case where this pass could enable further
--   optimizations. It can certainly destroy optimization opportunities,
--   similar to CSE. So it should only be run once, at the very end of the
--   pipeline. CSE is also run at the end, but it runs for multiple
--   iterations interleaved with other optimizations, because CSE can both
--   destroy and enable further optimizations.
module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.ApplyToCase
applyToCase :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. Monad m => Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m (Term name uni fun a)


-- | Hoist fully-forced polymorphic builtins.
--   
--   Before: <tt>...force (force fstPair)...</tt> After: <tt>(fstPairForced
--   -&gt; ...fstPairForced...) (force (force fstPair))</tt>
module UntypedPlutusCore.Transform.PolyBuiltin
polyBuiltin :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. (MonadQuote m, Hashable fun, ToBuiltinMeaning uni fun) => BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Term Name uni fun a -> OptimizerT Name uni fun a m (Term Name uni fun a)

module UntypedPlutusCore

-- | Completely replace the names with a new name type.
termMapNames :: forall name name' (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann. (name -> name') -> Term name uni fun ann -> Term name' uni fun ann
programMapNames :: forall name name' (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann. (name -> name') -> Program name uni fun ann -> Program name' uni fun ann
newtype InlineHints name a
InlineHints :: (a -> name -> Inline) -> InlineHints name a
[shouldInline] :: InlineHints name a -> a -> name -> Inline
data OptimizeOpts name a
OptimizeOpts :: Int -> Int -> CseWhichSubterms -> Bool -> InlineHints name a -> Bool -> AstSize -> AstSize -> Bool -> Bool -> Bool -> Bool -> OptimizeOpts name a
[_ooMaxSimplifierIterations] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> Int
[_ooMaxCseIterations] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> Int
[_ooCseWhichSubterms] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> CseWhichSubterms
[_ooConservativeOpts] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> Bool
[_ooInlineHints] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> InlineHints name a
[_ooInlineConstants] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> Bool
[_ooInlineUnconditionalGrowth] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> AstSize
[_ooInlineCallsiteGrowth] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> AstSize
[_ooPreserveLogging] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> Bool
[_ooApplyToCase] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> Bool
[_ooHoistPolyBuiltins] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> Bool
[_ooCertifiedOptsOnly] :: OptimizeOpts name a -> Bool
defaultOptimizeOpts :: OptimizeOpts name a

-- | Applicatively replace a variable using the given function.
substVarA :: forall f name ann (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. Applicative f => (name -> ann -> f (Maybe (Term name uni fun ann))) -> Term name uni fun ann -> f (Term name uni fun ann)

-- | Replace a variable using the given function.
substVar :: forall name ann (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. (name -> ann -> Maybe (Term name uni fun ann)) -> Term name uni fun ann -> Term name uni fun ann

-- | Naively monadically substitute names using the given function (i.e. do
--   not substitute binders).
termSubstNamesM :: forall m name ann (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. Monad m => (name -> ann -> m (Maybe (Term name uni fun ann))) -> Term name uni fun ann -> m (Term name uni fun ann)

-- | Naively substitute names using the given function (i.e. do not
--   substitute binders).
termSubstNames :: forall name ann (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. (name -> ann -> Maybe (Term name uni fun ann)) -> Term name uni fun ann -> Term name uni fun ann

-- | Get all the term variables in a term.
vTerm :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann f. (Contravariant f, Applicative f) => (name -> f name) -> Term name uni fun ann -> f (Term name uni fun ann)

-- | Applicatively replace a constant using the given function.
substConstantA :: forall f ann (uni :: Type -> Type) name fun. Applicative f => (ann -> Some (ValueOf uni) -> f (Maybe (Term name uni fun ann))) -> Term name uni fun ann -> f (Term name uni fun ann)

-- | Replace a constant using the given function.
substConstant :: forall ann (uni :: Type -> Type) name fun. (ann -> Some (ValueOf uni) -> Maybe (Term name uni fun ann)) -> Term name uni fun ann -> Term name uni fun ann

-- | Monadically substitute constants using the given function.
termSubstConstantsM :: forall m ann (uni :: Type -> Type) name fun. Monad m => (ann -> Some (ValueOf uni) -> m (Maybe (Term name uni fun ann))) -> Term name uni fun ann -> m (Term name uni fun ann)

-- | Substitute constants using the given function.
termSubstConstants :: forall ann (uni :: Type -> Type) name fun. (ann -> Some (ValueOf uni) -> Maybe (Term name uni fun ann)) -> Term name uni fun ann -> Term name uni fun ann
newtype OptimizerT name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann (m :: Type -> Type) a
OptimizerT :: StateT (OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann) m a -> OptimizerT name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann (m :: Type -> Type) a
[getOptimizerT] :: OptimizerT name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann (m :: Type -> Type) a -> StateT (OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann) m a
recordOptimization :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. Monad m => Term name uni fun a -> OptStage -> Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m ()
pattern ApplyToCaseStage :: OptStage
type Optimizer name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann = OptimizerT name uni fun ann Identity
pattern CaseOfCaseStage :: OptStage
pattern CaseReduceStage :: OptStage

-- | Which subterms should be considered as candidates for CSE?
data CseWhichSubterms
AllSubterms :: CseWhichSubterms
ExcludeWorkFree :: CseWhichSubterms
pattern CseStage :: OptStage
pattern FloatDelayStage :: OptStage
pattern ForceCaseDelayStage :: OptStage
pattern ForceDelayStage :: OptStage
recordOptimizationWithHints :: forall (m :: Type -> Type) name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. Monad m => Hints -> Term name uni fun a -> OptStage -> Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m ()
pattern InlineStage :: OptStage
pattern LetFloatOutStage :: OptStage
type OptStage = Either UncertifiedOptStage CertifiedOptStage
newtype OptimizerTrace name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
OptimizerTrace :: [Optimization name uni fun a] -> OptimizerTrace name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
[optimizerTrace] :: OptimizerTrace name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> [Optimization name uni fun a]
data Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
Optimization :: Term name uni fun a -> OptStage -> Hints -> Term name uni fun a -> Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a
[beforeAST] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> Term name uni fun a
[stage] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> OptStage
[hints] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> Hints
[afterAST] :: Optimization name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a -> Term name uni fun a
runOptimizerT :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann m a. OptimizerT name uni fun ann m a -> m (a, OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann)
evalOptimizerT :: forall m name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann a. Monad m => OptimizerT name uni fun ann m a -> m a
execOptimizerT :: forall m name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann a. Monad m => OptimizerT name uni fun ann m a -> m (OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann)
runOptimizer :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann a. Optimizer name uni fun ann a -> (a, OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann)
evalOptimizer :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann a. Optimizer name uni fun ann a -> a
execOptimizer :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann a. Optimizer name uni fun ann a -> OptimizerTrace name uni fun ann
initOptimizerTrace :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun a. OptimizerTrace name uni fun a
pattern PolyBuiltinStage :: OptStage
optimizeTerm :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun m a. Compiling m uni fun name a => OptimizeOpts name a -> BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Term name uni fun a -> m (Term name uni fun a)
optimizeProgram :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun m a. Compiling m uni fun name a => OptimizeOpts name a -> BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Program name uni fun a -> m (Program name uni fun a)
optimizeProgramWithTrace :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun m a. Compiling m uni fun name a => OptimizeOpts name a -> BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Program name uni fun a -> m (Program name uni fun a, OptimizerTrace name uni fun a)
termOptimizer :: forall name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun (m :: Type -> Type) a. Compiling m uni fun name a => OptimizeOpts name a -> BuiltinSemanticsVariant fun -> Term name uni fun a -> OptimizerT name uni fun a m (Term name uni fun a)
ooApplyToCase :: forall name a f. Functor f => (Bool -> f Bool) -> OptimizeOpts name a -> f (OptimizeOpts name a)
ooHoistPolyBuiltins :: forall name a f. Functor f => (Bool -> f Bool) -> OptimizeOpts name a -> f (OptimizeOpts name a)
ooMaxSimplifierIterations :: forall name a f. Functor f => (Int -> f Int) -> OptimizeOpts name a -> f (OptimizeOpts name a)
ooMaxCseIterations :: forall name a f. Functor f => (Int -> f Int) -> OptimizeOpts name a -> f (OptimizeOpts name a)
ooCseWhichSubterms :: forall name a f. Functor f => (CseWhichSubterms -> f CseWhichSubterms) -> OptimizeOpts name a -> f (OptimizeOpts name a)
ooInlineHints :: forall name1 a1 name2 a2 f. Functor f => (InlineHints name1 a1 -> f (InlineHints name2 a2)) -> OptimizeOpts name1 a1 -> f (OptimizeOpts name2 a2)
ooConservativeOpts :: forall name a f. Functor f => (Bool -> f Bool) -> OptimizeOpts name a -> f (OptimizeOpts name a)
ooInlineConstants :: forall name a f. Functor f => (Bool -> f Bool) -> OptimizeOpts name a -> f (OptimizeOpts name a)
ooInlineUnconditionalGrowth :: forall name a f. Functor f => (AstSize -> f AstSize) -> OptimizeOpts name a -> f (OptimizeOpts name a)
ooInlineCallsiteGrowth :: forall name a f. Functor f => (AstSize -> f AstSize) -> OptimizeOpts name a -> f (OptimizeOpts name a)
ooPreserveLogging :: forall name a f. Functor f => (Bool -> f Bool) -> OptimizeOpts name a -> f (OptimizeOpts name a)
ooCertifiedOptsOnly :: forall name a f. Functor f => (Bool -> f Bool) -> OptimizeOpts name a -> f (OptimizeOpts name a)
pattern ConstantFoldingStage :: OptStage

-- | The type of Untyped Plutus Core terms. Mirrors the type of Typed
--   Plutus Core terms except
--   
--   <ol>
--   <li>all types are removed</li>
--   <li><tt>IWrap</tt> and <tt>Unwrap</tt> are removed</li>
--   <li>type abstractions are replaced with <a>Delay</a></li>
--   <li>type instantiations are replaced with <a>Force</a></li>
--   </ol>
--   
--   The latter two are due to the fact that we don't have value
--   restriction in Typed Plutus Core and hence a computation can be stuck
--   expecting only a single type argument for the computation to become
--   unstuck. Therefore we can't just silently remove type abstractions and
--   instantiations and need to replace them with something else that also
--   blocks evaluation (in order for the semantics of an erased program to
--   match with the semantics of the original typed one). <a>Delay</a> and
--   <a>Force</a> serve exactly this purpose.
data Term name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
Var :: !ann -> !name -> Term name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
LamAbs :: !ann -> !name -> !Term name uni fun ann -> Term name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
Apply :: !ann -> !Term name uni fun ann -> !Term name uni fun ann -> Term name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
Force :: !ann -> !Term name uni fun ann -> Term name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
Delay :: !ann -> !Term name uni fun ann -> Term name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
Constant :: !ann -> !Some (ValueOf uni) -> Term name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
Builtin :: !ann -> !fun -> Term name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
Error :: !ann -> Term name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
Constr :: !ann -> !Word64 -> ![Term name uni fun ann] -> Term name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
Case :: !ann -> !Term name uni fun ann -> !Vector (Term name uni fun ann) -> Term name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann

-- | A <a>Program</a> is simply a <a>Term</a> coupled with a
--   <tt>Version</tt> of the core language.
data Program name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
Program :: ann -> Version -> Term name uni fun ann -> Program name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann
[_progAnn] :: Program name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann -> ann
[_progVer] :: Program name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann -> Version
[_progTerm] :: Program name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun ann -> Term name uni fun ann

-- | Applies one program to another. Fails if the versions do not match and
--   tries to merge annotations.
applyProgram :: forall m a name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. (MonadError ApplyProgramError m, Semigroup a) => Program name uni fun a -> Program name uni fun a -> m (Program name uni fun a)
applyTerm :: forall a name (uni :: Type -> Type) fun. Semigroup a => Term name uni fun a -> Term name uni fun a -> Term name uni fun a

-- | Parse and rewrite so that names are globally unique, not just unique
--   within their scope.
parseScoped :: forall (uni :: Type -> Type) fun m. (MonadError (Error uni fun SrcSpan) m, MonadQuote m) => Text -> m (Program Name DefaultUni DefaultFun SrcSpan)

-- | The universe used by default.
data DefaultUni a

-- | Default built-in functions.
--   
--   When updating these, make sure to add them to the protocol version
--   listing! See Note [New builtins/language versions and protocol
--   versions]
data DefaultFun

module PlutusCore.Compiler

-- | Compile a PLC term to UPLC, and optimize it.
compileTerm :: forall m (uni :: Type -> Type) fun name a tyname. (Compiling m uni fun name a, MonadReader (CompilationOpts name fun a) m) => Term tyname name uni fun a -> m (Term name uni fun a)

-- | Compile a PLC program to UPLC, and optimize it.
compileProgram :: forall m (uni :: Type -> Type) fun name a tyname. (Compiling m uni fun name a, MonadReader (CompilationOpts name fun a) m) => Program tyname name uni fun a -> m (Program name uni fun a)

-- | Compile a PLC program to UPLC, and optimize it. This includes the
--   compilation trace in the result.
compileProgramWithTrace :: forall m (uni :: Type -> Type) fun name a tyname. (Compiling m uni fun name a, MonadReader (CompilationOpts name fun a) m) => Program tyname name uni fun a -> m (Program name uni fun a, OptimizerTrace name uni fun a)
